purpose: Hand-checked candidate links from the hand-authored math-v0 corpus (profile-M v0, typed Peano basis) to set.mm counterparts in the math-mm bulk corpus. These are ANNOTATION-layer bridge candidates (sameConceptAs-style, per design-math-sector.md SS1.2's bridge-target doctrine), NOT identity claims: set.mm is untyped ZFC, so most counterparts carry von-Neumann/Kuratowski ENCODING RESIDUE that profile-M's typed basis deliberately excludes from concept identity (design-math-sector.md SS2.3). Nothing here is endorsed.
method: manual review by the extracting agent (2026-07-07) against the pinned set.mm statements; only links the agent is confident of are listed; confidence and caveats are per-link. Omitted deliberately: math-v0's axiom records (set.mm's Peano facts - peano1..peano5, nsuceq0 - are THEOREMS ($p), out of this corpus's $a-only scope), predecessor (no clean df counterpart), the integers-as-equivalence-classes sketch (set.mm defines ZZ as a subset of RR, df-z, not as pair-classes), and comprehension-adjacent primitives beyond set-of.

alignments.note: N as omega (finite ordinals; om-side counterpart). Encoding residue: naturals are von Neumann ordinals. Complex-number-side alternate: urn:math-mm:df-n0 (NN0, includes 0; NN of df-nn starts at 1).
zero as (/) (omega's zero). Encoding residue: zero IS the empty set there. Complex-number-side alternate: urn:math-mm:df-0 (0 of CC, constructed).
suc A = ( A u. { A } ) - von Neumann successor; encoding residue in the definiens, same role on omega.
1o = suc (/) - structurally succ(zero), exactly math-v0's one. Complex-number-side alternate: urn:math-mm:df-1.
2 = ( 1 + 1 ) over CC - structurally identical to math-v0's two := succ(one) modulo which addition grounds it. Ordinal-side alternate: urn:math-mm:df-2o (2o = suc 1o).
3 = ( 2 + 1 ) over CC. Ordinal-side: df-3o does not exist; 3o unused in set.mm main body.

+o : ordinal addition by transfinite recursion (rec of suc) - the structural counterpart of math-v0's primitive recursion; on omega it IS Peano addition. Everyday-arithmetic alternate: urn:math-mm:df-add (+ on CC, iota-based, not recursive).
x o ordinal multiplication; on omega it is Peano multiplication. Alternate: urn:math-mm:df-mul (x. on CC).

class equality (extensional, one untyped sort, and itself part of set.mm's documented df-cleq/df-clel definitional 2-cycle) vs math-v0's typed primitive eq per sort. Role-counterpart, not meaning-equal.
class membership vs math-v0's typed member at Set(sigma). Same caveats as equality.
class abstraction { x | ph } vs math-v0's typed comprehension former. set.mm's is proper-class-capable and untyped.
< on RR* (the arithmetic order actually used); restricted to naturals it agrees with math-v0's less-than. Omega-side counterpart is bare membership (df-eprel as a relation), which is pure encoding.

<_ = complement of converse < on RR*; agrees on naturals. math-v0 defines via E. k ( x + k ) = y - a different (provably equivalent on N) formulation; distinct formulations mint distinct ids by design (design-math-sector.md SS3.4).
|| over ZZ (math-v0's is over N; agree on N).
MATHBOX record (AV); Even = { z e. ZZ | ( z / 2 ) e. ZZ } - over ZZ and via division, vs math-v0's divides-based over N. Agree on N.
MATHBOX record (AV); same caveats as even.
Prime = { p e. NN | { n e. NN | n || p } ~~ 2o } - two-divisor formulation vs math-v0's no-nontrivial-divisor formulation; provably equivalent, formulation-distinct.

(/) - untyped vs math-v0's Set(N)-typed empty set. Note df-nul is also the zero alignment target: in ZFC-encoding they are the SAME object; in profile-M they are deliberately distinct concepts (sort N vs Set(N)) - this collision is itself a clean exhibit of encoding residue.
{ A } untyped vs typed at N.
C_ untyped vs typed at Set(N).
u. binary union.
i^i binary intersection.
<. A , B >. - Kuratowski encoding residue vs math-v0's Pair primitive.
1st via U. dom { A } machinery - encoding residue; same role.
2nd; as pair-first.
F : A --> B (total function class) vs math-v0's functions-as-graphs predicate over Set(Pair(N,N)). Role-counterpart.
F : A -1-1-> B; as function-on-nat.
